description:
Umbrella for all Phase 1 work. All existing 148 tests must continue to pass.

Branch: system-test-tcp
Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge.
All changes are in tools/system-test-validator/src/ (wire.rs, harness.rs) — no production rawket changes.
title: Phase 1: Non-breaking infrastructure

close_reason: establish_pair_with_link now calls drain_flight() before each handshake poll to deliver frames through the delay queue. Works with both instant and non-instant link profiles. 148/148 pass.
description: establish_pair_with_link() 3-way handshake doesn't work with non-instant profiles. The polls (B→A→B) don't advance clocks between them, so frames sit in the delay queue and poll(2) times out. Fix: insert advance_a_to_b() / advance_b_to_a() calls between handshake polls.
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: establish_pair_with_link handshake needs clock advancement

close_reason: Added pause_both() and resume_both() to Pair. 148/148 pass.
description: 9 tests use pair.clock.pause() to freeze time for deterministic RTO/timeout testing. With independent clocks, they need to pause both clocks. Add pause_both() / resume_both() helpers to Pair. Tests: teardown (2), rto_rtt (6), state_machine (1).
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: Pair pause/resume helpers for independent clocks

close_reason: Added advance_a_to_b_for(frame_len) and advance_b_to_a_for(frame_len). Original advance_a_to_b/advance_b_to_a delegate to them with MSS/ACK defaults. 148/148 pass.
description: advance_a_to_b() always uses MSS_FRAME_LEN (1514 bytes) for serialization delay, but actual frames vary (SYN=74B, ACK=54B, data=variable). With slow links (10 Mbps) the difference matters: MSS=0.12ms vs SYN=0.006ms. Consider adding advance_a_to_b_with_len(len) or just document that the fixed size is intentional for typical data exchange.
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: advance_a_to_b frame size is fixed at MSS_FRAME_LEN

close_reason: Switched delivery model from sender clock to receiver clock. drain_flight now advances clock_b for A→B and clock_a for B→A (receiver side). deliver_ready and rx_recv lazy delivery also use receiver clocks. Sender clocks are never modified by frame delivery. 148/148 pass.
description: drain_flight() advances the sender's clock to the max delivery_time. This clock jump could trigger RTO/timer behavior in subsequent polls. Tests need to be aware of this side effect. Consider documenting the behavior or adding a warning, or making drain_flight not advance the sender clock beyond what's needed.
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: drain_flight advances sender clock which may trigger unexpected timers

close_reason: virtual_tx now checks loss_rate after delay computation. Uses xorshift64 PRNG seeded from clock. Dropped frames are marked in CaptureBuffer (last_mut added). All presets except lossy_wan have loss_rate=0 so no behavior change. 148/148 pass.
description: DirectionProfile has loss_rate field but virtual_tx doesn't check it. Design says 'Applied independently to each frame after delay computation.' Needed for lossy_wan() preset. No Phase 2 tests likely need it, but the feature is incomplete.
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: loss_rate not implemented in virtual_tx

close_reason: virtual_tx now applies uniform [0, jitter_ms] random jitter to delivery_time. All presets have jitter_ms=0 so no behavior change. 148/148 pass.
description: DirectionProfile has jitter_ms field but virtual_tx doesn't apply it. Design says 'Uniform distribution [0, jitter_ms]. 0 = deterministic.' No Phase 2 tests likely need it, but the feature is incomplete. Would need a PRNG source (no std rand available in no_std context — test-only so can use libc random or simple LCG).
notes: Commit strategy: --fixup d022b8e (harness introduction commit), squashed before merge. Harness-only change (wire.rs / harness.rs), no test implementation changes.
title: jitter_ms not implemented in virtual_tx

description:
- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Test advances clock past `rto_max_ms` before measuring the retransmit gap,
    making the gap assertion meaningless -- can't distinguish recovered RTO from
    inflated RTO. The second-phase clock advance should be just past `rto_min_ms`
    (not `rto_max_ms`) to verify the RTO recovered via timestamps.
---

description: Add three bool fields to NetworkConfig for per-protocol RX checksum validation (IP, TCP, UDP). Default false — matching current behavior where AF_PACKET frames arrive post-offload. Fields: checksum_validate_ip, checksum_validate_tcp, checksum_validate_udp.
title: Checksum validation settings

description:
Add three bool fields (checksum_validate_ip, checksum_validate_tcp, checksum_validate_udp) with default() = false. Mirror in RawketNetworkConfig (FFI) and the From conversion. Update rawket_network_config_default(). Thread through Uplink to Interface via a setter (like existing set_icmp_rate_limit).

Files: src/network.rs, src/ffi.rs, include/rawket.h, src/interface.rs
title: Add checksum config fields to NetworkConfig

description:
In udp::dispatch() (udp.rs ~264), when checksum_validate_udp is set, compute pseudo-header + UDP checksum and reject frames where it's non-zero. Handle the RFC 768 special case (checksum field = 0 means 'no checksum'). Pass the flag from Interface to the dispatch call.

Files: src/udp.rs, src/interface.rs
title: Add UDP checksum validation

description:
In tcp::dispatch() and Uplink::process_standalone_tcp(), when checksum_validate_tcp is set, compute pseudo-header + TCP checksum and reject frames where it's non-zero. The checksum utilities already exist in ip.rs.

Files: src/tcp.rs, src/network.rs, src/interface.rs
title: Add TCP checksum validation

description:
Currently always validated in Ipv4Hdr::parse(). Add a skip_checksum variant or a separate parse_unchecked() for when checksum_validate_ip = false. When disabled, skip the checksum check to avoid false failures on offloaded frames. Default false = skip validation (matching current behavior for UDP/TCP; note: this changes IP from always-validate to default-off for consistency).

Files: src/ip.rs, src/interface.rs
title: Make IPv4 checksum validation configurable

close_reason: Added Pair helpers: advance_a_to_b(), advance_b_to_a(), advance_rtt(), advance_both(ms), drain_flight(). WireHarness gained deliver_ready() and drain_flight(). DelayQueue gained max_delivery_time(). 148/148 tests pass.
description:
Add methods to Pair in harness.rs:
- advance_a_to_b() — advance clock_b by A→B one-way delay (MSS-sized frame)
- advance_b_to_a() — advance clock_a by B→A one-way delay (ACK-sized frame)
- advance_rtt() — advance both clocks by full RTT
- drain_flight() — advance clocks enough to deliver all in-flight frames
- advance_both(ms) — advance both clocks by same amount (legacy compat)

Each helper also calls deliver_ready() to flush deliverable frames.
title: Pair advancement helpers

description:
Evolve the system test wire from instant/shared-clock to a realistic link model
with per-direction latency/bandwidth and independent clocks per side.

Design doc: designs/system-test-plan/network-simulation-design.md

## Problem
- Shared Clock::default() + clone means both Network stacks see identical time
- VirtualLink delivers frames instantly (zero latency, infinite bandwidth)
- Tests use magic `advance(5)` constants with no physical meaning
- BBR can't estimate bandwidth or detect congestion with zero RTT
- Pacing is untestable — frames arrive before pacing timer fires

## Solution
- LinkProfile with per-direction latency, bandwidth, jitter, loss
- Independent clocks per side (call Clock::default() twice)
- Delay queues in WireHarness gating frame delivery on sender clock
- Link-aware Pair helpers: advance_a_to_b(), advance_rtt(), drain_flight()
- 3-phase migration preserving all 148 existing tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LinkProfile + DirectionProfile structs with preset profiles
- [ ] Independent clocks in Pair (clock_a, clock_b)
- [ ] Delay queues in WireHarness per direction
- [ ] Frame delivery gated on sender clock
- [ ] Pair advancement helpers replace magic constants
- [ ] All existing tests pass with LinkProfile::instant()
- [ ] At least one test uses realistic link profile (e.g. broadband)
- [ ] inter_packet_gap_matches_rate rewritten with link model
title: Network Simulation: realistic link model for system tests

description:
- type: task
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Several rack_tlp tests can't distinguish RACK-driven retransmits from RTO-driven ones:
    - `multiple_losses_detected`: use paused clock + precise advances to ensure retransmits
      happen before RTO, or check `rack_end_seq` advanced
    - `rack_updates_from_sack`: inspect `rack_end_seq` / `rack_xmit_ms` directly
    - `rack_reo_wnd_decay`: assert strict `<` (not `<=`), assert `reo_wnd_after_loss > 0`
    - `rack_loss_triggers_bbr_on_loss`: compare cwnd before/after, not just `<= 20*MSS`
---
title: Strengthen WEAK: RACK-TLP mechanism discrimination

description:
- type: epic
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Audit of all 148 system tests found 82 SOLID, 56 WEAK, and 10 INVALID tests.
    INVALID tests don't validate the requirement their name claims. WEAK tests have
    loose bounds, vacuous guards, or can't distinguish the mechanism under test from
    fallback behavior (e.g., RACK vs RTO).
    Strongest modules: keepalive (8/8), persist (7/8), mss (10/11), teardown (8/11).
    Weakest modules: bbr (2/15 solid), pacing (0/6 solid), rack_tlp (4/10 solid).
    This epic tracks fixing the INVALID tests first, then tightening WEAK assertions
    by cluster. Some fixes depend on the network simulation epic (rawket-aeh) for
    realistic timing measurement.
---
title: Strengthen system test assertions

description:
- type: task
- priority: 2
- description: |
    11 of 15 BBR tests are weak smoke tests that never inspect `bbr_phase()`, `bw_lo`,
    `inflight_lo`, or actual cwnd growth patterns. Specific fixes:
    - `startup_phase_grows_cwnd`: sample cwnd at multiple points, assert monotonic increase
    - `startup_to_drain_transition`: assert `bbr_phase()` passes through Startup -> Drain -> ProbeBw
    - `probe_bw_up_down_cycle`: check phase is ProbeBw, inspect pacing gain changes
    - `probe_rtt_entry`: assert `bbr_phase() == ProbeRtt` after 10s advance
    - `loss_triggers_bw_lo_reduction`: read `bw_lo` before/after, assert decrease
    - `probe_rtt_cwnd_reduced`: assert `cwnd <= 4*MSS` while in ProbeRtt (check phase first)
    - `loss_halves_inflight_lo`: don't accept sentinel; assert actual halving
    - `bw_sample_window`: use a meaningful threshold, not `>= 1000`
    - `cwnd_target_includes_bdp`: compute expected BDP, compare against cwnd
    - `min_rtt_tracking`: assert value correlates with simulated RTT
    May need additional `test-internals` accessors for `bw_lo`, `inflight_lo`, `pacing_gain`.
---
title: Strengthen WEAK: BBR phase and internal state assertions

close_reason: virtual_tx now pushes frames to DelayQueue with delivery_time computed from DirectionProfile, then eagerly delivers ready frames. rx_recv does lazy delivery before reading. Corrupt impairment integrated into same flow. self_pipe_w added to VirtualLink. 148/148 tests pass.
description:
Modify virtual_tx() in wire.rs:
- After impairment processing (drop/corrupt), compute delivery_time from DirectionProfile
- Push to DelayQueue instead of directly to peer rx_queue
- Apply loss_rate check before enqueue
- Record send_time + delivery_time in CaptureBuffer metadata
- deliver_ready() called from rx_recv() (lazy) and from Pair helpers (eager)

With LinkProfile::instant(), behavior is identical to current (delivery_time = now).
title: Wire virtual_tx to use delay queues

close_reason: All 81 test functions migrated to link-aware Pair helpers; harness fixed for independent clocks; 148/148 tests pass
description:
Convert all tests using pair.clock.advance() to link-aware Pair helpers.

Each sub-bead is one test function. Changes are --fixup commits onto the commit
that introduced that test.

Categories of advance() usage:
1. Poll-loop pacing (advance(5) between polls) → advance_a_to_b() / advance_b_to_a()
2. Timeout triggers (advance(2000), advance(60_000)) → advance_both(N)
3. BBR/pacing tests → also switch to establish_pair_with_link(LinkProfile::broadband())
title: Phase 2: Test migration

close_reason: Fixed in cd7887f + 69c86b6. 148/148 pass.
notes:
advance_both() calls clock_a.advance() then clock_b.advance(). When setup_pair()
creates a shared clock (clock_a and clock_b are the same Rc<ClockInner>), this
doubles the time advance. Same issue with pause_both() and resume_both().

Fix: add Clock::same_instance(&self, &other) -> bool using Rc::ptr_eq, then
guard the second advance/pause/resume call in advance_both/pause_both/resume_both.

Two commits:
1. Fixup into harness commit: add same_instance() to Clock + fix Pair helpers
2. Resolve any test regressions caused by the double-advance

Currently flaky (not consistently failing) because the double-advance only
manifests when timing-sensitive tests run under system load.
title: Fix advance_both / pause_both double-advance in shared-clock mode

description:
- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    With `max_retransmits=4` and 10 iterations each advancing by `rto_max_ms`,
    the socket is Closed before the assertion runs. The `if state != Closed` guard
    silently skips the check. Fix: use a higher `max_retransmits` or fewer iterations,
    and assert the check actually ran.
---

description:
- type: task
- priority: 3
- description: |
    Tests where the name/comments claim something the assertions don't check:
    - `sack::most_recent_block_first`: actually check block ordering per RFC 2018 s4
    - `sack::ooo_buffer_limit`: check final ACK's SACK blocks, not union across all ACKs
    - `ooo::ooo_duplicate_seq`: verify D-SACK block, not just delivery
    - `ooo::ooo_segment_overlaps_rcv_nxt`: test partial overlap, not fully-old segment
    - `teardown::fin_piggybacked_on_data`: assert FIN+data in same segment
    - `teardown::half_close_data_delivery`: verify A's ACK advances past B's data
    - `teardown::syn_during_time_wait`: don't accept Closed as valid
    - `send_buf::flow_control_window_grows`: assert windows actually increase over time
    - `timestamps_paws::paws_drop_stale`: use in-window seq with stale TSval
    - `timestamps_paws::syn_bypasses_paws`: need prior ts_recent state
    - `isn::syn_collision_ack_numbers`: unwrap instead of `if let`
    - `isn::isn_epoch_collision`: reuse same Pair to test epoch-based ISN
    - `persist::persist_exponential_backoff`: verify inter-probe gap doubling
title: Strengthen WEAK: name vs. reality mismatches

description: Enable the new checksum validation flags in system tests since VirtualLink has no hardware offload. Depends on Epic 1 (rawket-597) completing first.
title: Enable checksum validation in system tests

description:
Set all three checksum flags to true in fast_tcp_cfg() and the system test harness since VirtualLink has no offload. This also fixes bad_checksum_dropped (currently INVALID in rawket-e7dd).

Files: tools/system-test-validator/src/harness.rs
title: Enable checksums in system test harness

description:
Test that bad IP, TCP, and UDP checksums are dropped when validation is enabled, and accepted when disabled. Use VirtualLink with corrupted frames.

Files: tools/system-test-validator/src/tcp/state_machine.rs (or new file)
title: Add checksum validation tests

description:
- type: task
- priority: 3
- description: |
    Cross-cutting pattern of assertions that are technically correct but too loose
    to catch regressions:
    State machine:
    - `rst_with_invalid_seq`: also assert no error was set
    - `syn_retransmit`: count retransmitted SYNs, not just final state
    - `rst_in_window_challenge_ack`: verify challenge ACK seq/ack values, not just `any(ACK)`
    - `challenge_ack_rate_limit`: add lower bound `ack_count > 0`
    - `send_in_non_established`: require error return, don't accept success
    ECN:
    - `ce_marking_ece_cwr`: also check CWR on next data segment from sender
    - `ece_triggers_cwnd_reduction`: assert `cwnd_after <= cwnd_before` (not `*2`)
    Fast retransmit:
    - `partial_ack`: verify snd_una advanced
    - `triggers_bbr_loss`: compare cwnd before/after loss
    RTO/RTT:
    - `timestamps_override_karn`: tighter bounds on srtt/rto in zero-latency env
    - `rto_update_convergence`: handle min_srtt==0 case; use meaningful convergence check
---
title: Strengthen WEAK: loose bounds and vacuous guards

description:
Cleanup tasks discovered during Phase 2 migration.

Sub-beads will be added here as Phase 2 work reveals specific cleanup needed:
- Remove deprecated pair.clock field
- Remove Impairment::Delay variant (replaced by LinkProfile)
- Audit: no raw advance() calls remain in test bodies (only in Pair helpers)
- Any other cleanup that shakes out during migration

This bead stays deferred until Phase 2 (rawket-gs2) is complete.
title: Phase 3: Cleanup

description:
- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Assertion accepts `State::Established` as valid (`b_state == CloseWait || Established`),
    making the FIN-processing check vacuous. After OOO drain with the FIN, B must be in
    CloseWait. Remove the `Established` alternative or add sufficient poll rounds to ensure
    the FIN is processed before asserting.
---
title: Fix INVALID: ooo::fin_in_ooo

description:
- type: bug
- priority: 1
- description: |
    Test claims to verify RFC 8985 section 7.3 (TLP suppressed when RTO fires sooner)
    but only checks liveness. Need to configure timers so RTO < TLP (e.g., rto_min=5ms
    with higher SRTT making TLP ~20ms), then verify the retransmit was RTO-driven
    (check timing matches RTO, not TLP interval) or that no TLP probe was scheduled.
---

description:
- type: bug
- priority: 2
- description: |
    Three pacing tests are INVALID:
    - `inter_packet_gap_matches_rate`: never measures inter-packet timing
    - `probe_bw_up_increases_rate`: no rate measurement or phase check
    - `probe_bw_down_decreases_rate`: no rate measurement or phase check
    These require the network simulation epic (rawket-aeh) for realistic link timing.
    Until then, rewrite to at least check BBR phase transitions and pacing_rate values
    via test-internals accessors. Mark timing-dependent assertions as deferred.
- notes: |
    Depends on rawket-aeh for full fix. Partial fix possible now using phase/rate accessors.
---
title: Fix INVALID: pacing tests (3 tests)
